briefdescription.para: @SINCE_1_0.0
Enumeration for BaseType. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Size type @SINCE_1_0.0.
Most simple Iterator is a pointer @SINCE_1_0.0.

memberdef.definition: using Dali::Vector< T, IsTrivialType >::ConstIterator = const T*
memberdef.name: ConstIterator

briefdescription.para: Const iterator @SINCE_1_0.0.
Item type @SINCE_1_0.0.
Default constructor. Does not allocate any space. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Create vector with count, without initialize value.

detaileddescription.para:
Only able for trivial types.
@SINCE_2_3.22
count
The count of vector.

briefdescription.para: Destructor. Releases the allocated space. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Copy constructor.
Default move constructor.
Assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
vector
Vector to assign from
Reference to self for chaining

briefdescription.para: Default move assignment operator.
Iterator to the beginning of the data. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: Iterator to the beginning of the data

briefdescription.para: Iterator to the end of the data (one past last element). @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: Iterator to the end of the data (one past last element)

detaileddescription.para:
Support for C++11 Range-based for loop: for( item : container ). @SINCE_1_2.60
The start iterator

Support for C++11 Range-based for loop: for( item : container ). @SINCE_1_2.60
The end iterator

briefdescription.para: Subscript operator. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: Reference to the element for given index
Index must be in the vector's range.

briefdescription.para: Subscript operator. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: Reference to the element for given index
Index must be in the vector's range.

briefdescription.para: Get the first element of vector. @SINCE_2_5.17.
Get the first element of vector. @SINCE_2_5.17.
Get the last element of vector. @SINCE_2_5.17.
Get the last element of vector. @SINCE_2_5.17.
Pushes back an element to the vector.

detaileddescription.para: The underlying storage may be reallocated to provide space. If this occurs, all pre-existing pointers into the vector will become invalid.

briefdescription.para: Pushes back an element to the vector using move semantics.

detaileddescription.para: The underlying storage may be reallocated to provide space. If this occurs, all pre-existing pointers into the vector will become invalid.

briefdescription.para: Inserts an element to the vector.

detaileddescription.para: Elements after at are moved one position to the right.
The underlying storage may be reallocated to provide space. If this occurs, all pre-existing pointers into the vector will become invalid.

@SINCE_1_0.0
at
Iterator where to insert the elements into the vector
element
An element to be added
Iterator at must be in the vector's range ( Vector::Begin(), Vector::End() ).

briefdescription.para: Inserts an element to the vector using move semantics.

detaileddescription.para: Elements after at are moved one position to the right.
The underlying storage may be reallocated to provide space. If this occurs, all pre-existing pointers into the vector will become invalid.

@SINCE_2_5.16
at
Iterator where to insert the element into the vector
element
An element to be moved and added
Iterator at must be in the vector's range ( Vector::Begin(), Vector::End() ).

briefdescription.para: Inserts the given elements into the vector.

detaileddescription.para: Elements after at are moved the number of given elements positions to the right.
The underlying storage may be reallocated to provide space. If this occurs, all pre-existing pointers into the vector will become invalid.

@SINCE_1_0.0
at
Iterator where to insert the elements into the vector
from
Iterator to the first element to be inserted
to
Iterator to the last element to be inserted
Iterator at must be in the vector's range ( Vector::Begin(), Vector::End() ).
Iterators from and to must be valid iterators.
Iterator from must not be grater than Iterator to.

briefdescription.para: Reserves space in the vector.

detaileddescription.para:
Reserving less than current Capacity is a no-op. @SINCE_1_0.0
count
Count of elements to reserve

briefdescription.para: Resizes the vector. Does not change capacity.
Resizes the vector without initializing the data.

detaileddescription.para:
Can be used as a data container for reading whole file content.
Only able for trivial types.
@SINCE_1_9.27
count
Count to resize to

briefdescription.para: Resizes the vector. Does not change capacity.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
count
Count to resize to
item
An item to insert to the new indices

briefdescription.para: Erases an element.

detaileddescription.para: Does not change capacity. Other elements get moved.

@SINCE_1_0.0
iterator
Iterator pointing to the item to remove
Iterator pointing to next element
Iterator iterator must be within the vector's range ( Vector::Begin(), Vector::End() - 1 ).

briefdescription.para: Erases a range of elements.

detaileddescription.para: Does not change capacity. Other elements get moved.

@SINCE_1_0.0
first
Iterator to the first element to be erased
last
Iterator to the last element to be erased
Iterator pointing to the next element of the last one
Iterator first must be in the vector's range ( Vector::Begin(), Vector::End() ).
Iterator last must be in the vector's range ( Vector::Begin(), Vector::End() ).
Iterator first must not be grater than Iterator last.

briefdescription.para: Removes an element.

detaileddescription.para: Does not maintain order. Swaps the element with end and decreases size by one. This is much faster than Erase so use this in case order does not matter. Does not change capacity.

@SINCE_1_0.0
iterator
Iterator pointing to the item to remove
Iterator iterator must be in the vector's range ( Vector::Begin(), Vector::End() - 1 ).

briefdescription.para: Swaps the contents of two vectors.
Clears the contents of the vector. Keeps its capacity. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Releases the memory that the vector holds. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Fit the capacity of vector as item counts.
Fit the capacity of vector as item counts only if low spec memory management enabled, and the count is much smaller than capacity. It will be used when we want to remove unused memory.
Vector class with minimum space allocation when it's empty.
